assumptions: The recognised dividend receivable is an intragroup monetary item denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure between entities with different functional currencies.
That foreign exchange exposure would give rise to exchange differences that are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge of foreign currency risk on an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: This is a genuine hedge designation route because IFRS 9 allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to be designated in consolidated financial statements when the exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
